message.content:
Adversarially verify this reverse-engineered architecture claim. Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot
confirm it from a PRIMARY source (paper, repo, official doc) or from the artifact text itself.

SYSTEM: bodhi = "Bodhi Deep Research", the research engine inside Sapient Bodhi, the enterprise agentic-AI platform from Publicis Sapient (a Western consultancy, NOT a Chinese team -- despite writing native Chinese for Chinese prompts). GPT-5.5 board: 54.07 overall, rank 3.
HAS PAPER: false
ARCHITECTURE CLAIMED: PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially nothing. No arXiv paper, no GitHub, no HF model/dataset card, no tech report, no Chinese-language writeup (I searched 深度研究/智能体/报告生成 -- zero hits; it is not a Chinese team). The ONLY public statements are marketing: the product page, a Threads post, and LinkedIn posts. The full technical content of the LinkedIn post is: "The reasoning engine behind Sapient Bodhi is now ranked #3 in the world... Bodhi Deep Research powers multiple enterprise agents across the platform." Product pages add only: multi-agent, an "Enterprise Context Graph" shared-context layer, shared memory, model-agnostic routing, runs on Amazon Bedrock + AgentCore. NOTE a live contradiction in their own materials: the product page claims "#1 spot on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard" while their own social posts say #3 -- treat all their marketing numbers as unreliable. Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure.

ARTIFACT-DERIVED ARCHITECTURE (n=100, gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl):

(1) RETRIEVE-FIRST, PRE-NUMBERED GLOBAL SOURCE LIBRARY. Citations render as [[n]](url). In 99/99 reports each index n maps to exactly ONE url -- a stable global index. Mean max index = 128.7 (median 121, max 280), but only 61.3 unique URLs are actually cited: ~50% of numbered slots are NEVER used. In 47/99 reports the first citation in the document is not [1]. So numbering is NOT order-of-first-appearance -- it is assigned by a research phase that runs BEFORE composition, which builds a ~130-280-item numbered library, of which the writer selects about half. Over-retrieve, then curate.

(2) THE COMPOSER HOLDS THE WHOLE LIBRARY (not section-scoped retrieval). Decisive test: correlation between position-in-document and citation index. bodhi = +0.279, and the first 20% of the document already draws on 83% of the report's full index range. Compare WhaleCloud-DocChain = +0.872 with only 27% of the range used in the first 20% -- that is the signature of section-by-section composition with sources appended as they are encountered. bodhi's near-flat correlation means the writer had the entire source pool addressable from sentence one. (sourcery is even flatter, +0.018/96%.)

(3) SCOPE CONTRACT AS SECTION ZERO. Reports open not with an executive summary but with a definitional/measurement-convention contract: it states its own assumptions, date cutoff, definition of the key term, and its evidence policy. Verbatim examples: "Scope, assumptions, and how 'AI-driven restructuring' is treated in this review... Evidence constraint: I only cite journal articles"; "以下报告在...做两点假设并据此展开"; "我会在对应条目中明确'公司自述/媒体转述/监管披露'的证据强度" (I will explicitly mark evidence strength as company-self-report vs media vs regulatory-filing). First H2 is very often a 口径 (measurement-convention) section, e.g. "1) 数据口径与'为什么你可能看到不同的历史高点'". This is almost certainly what drives instruction_following 54.41.

(4) BESPOKE OUTLINE, ARGUMENTATIVE HEADINGS. 1186 H2s across 100 reports; the most-repeated H2 appears only 4 times. There is NO boilerplate outline. Headings carry claims, not topics: "不是'谁热'，而是谁能穿越周期" (not who's hot, but who survives the cycle), "谁真有空间，谁只是表面热闹" (who has real room and who is just superficially hot). ~11.9 H2 / 29.1 H3 per report.

(5) GENRE-ADAPTIVE FORMATTING. 66/100 reports contain ZERO table rows. Tables appear only when the task demands a comparison matrix (横向比较, "evaluate their input signals and accuracy", hardware-PM comparison). Contrast WhaleCloud (135 table rows/report avg) and cellcog (87) which table everything. Length variance is huge: 9k to 147k chars, stdev/mean = 0.56. bodhi sizes and formats to the task, not to a target.

(6) LENGTH IS NOT THE LEVER. bodhi is the SHORTEST of the top group (~10.7k words vs cellcog 23.9k, gemini 16.9k) and still beats gemini by 4 points. Within bodhi's own 100, corr(length, insight) = +0.05 and corr(length, readability) = -0.21. Insight-per-word, not word count.

(7) OUTPUT IS A BRIEF, NOT AN ESSAY. 53% of lines are bullets, only 25% prose lines, mean prose line 260 chars. It does NOT rely on named-study-in-prose (0.78/report, the LOWEST of the pack -- cellcog is 2.96). The Brynjolfsson/Acemoglu prose in task 72 is an exception the literature-review task forced.

(8) NO REVISION/CRITIC PASS IS VISIBLE, and no reference list: 92/100 have no bibliography section (citations are inline hyperlinks only). Consistency of index-to-URL mapping and the tight score band (insight 0.483-0.635) suggest a disciplined single composition pass over a curated library, rather than a critique-and-rewrite loop. UNVERIFIED -- absence of evidence.
COMPOSITION METHOD CLAIMED: THE ANSWER: bodhi writes in TWO EXPLICITLY DEMARCATED REGISTERS, and the synthesis lives entirely in the second one.

Register A -- EVIDENCE. Densely cited: 4.97 citations per 1000 chars, and 72% of substantive sentences carry at least one [[n]](url). Each of ~61 cited sources is re-cited ~4.06 times. THIS IS THE CRITICAL POINT FOR US: bodhi is NOT less grounded than POLARIS. It grounds at roughly the same rate we do. Grounding density is not the difference.

Register B -- INFERENCE, marked by a bolded label. I extracted every line of the form `**Label**: text`. Lines whose label is an inference word (implication / 含义 / 机制 / 解读 / 判断 / why / takeaway / interpretation) are UNCITED 67% of the time (137 uncited vs 69 cited). Lines with any other label -- the evidence register -- are uncited only 32% of the time. That 2x gap is the whole architecture. The bold label LICENSES an uncited, cross-source inferential sentence.

Real examples of Register B (all uncited):
- "**Restructuring implication**: even when aggregate employment effects are small in the short run, firms may still be actively reorganizing work -- shifting hiring away from some roles, rewriting skill requirements..."
- "**统一解读** (unified interpretation): the metal is still Au and the work function did not change, yet merely reducing contamination/damage (UHV) pushed Rc down -- this directly proves that work-function matching is NOT the first-order determinant; interface states / residue / damage-induced pinning are."
- "**机制差异**: Europe/US are more mature in open licensing, APIs and open-data ecosystems; China is stronger on national platformization and infrastructure-ization, but reusable learning objects + open licensing + data interoperability remain the next-stage bottleneck."
That second one is precisely the move POLARIS forbids: it reads several papers and states what they JOINTLY prove. No single span supports it.

THE LABELS ARE PER-REPORT, NOT A GLOBAL TEMPLATE. 1720 DISTINCT bolded labels across 100 reports (1955 uses). Within one report a label recurs (统一解读 x11, 传导机制 x10, 为什么它强 x13, Restructuring implication x5) but across reports they are almost all unique. So bodhi INVENTS A TASK-SPECIFIC ANALYTIC RUBRIC per report -- a small set of named analytic slots -- then applies it to every evidence unit. That is what makes the synthesis feel systematic rather than decorative: the same 2-4 questions get asked of every piece of evidence.

HOW IT HANDLES CONFLICTING EVIDENCE -- COUNTERINTUITIVE. bodhi has the FEWEST conflict markers of the top systems (4.5/report vs cellcog 12.4, gemini 8.3, WhaleCloud 8.6) and only 3/100 have a "limitations" heading. It does NOT have a tensions section. Instead it dissolves conflict ARCHITECTURALLY: the opening 口径/definitions section explains WHY sources disagree (different measurement conventions), so the entire report is organized as a reconciliation. It also grades sources by type in-line (company-self-report vs media vs regulatory filing) rather than flagging disagreement.

WHERE SYNTHESIS SITS. Two places, both non-standard. (a) Distributed: an inference line after nearly every evidence unit -- synthesis is continuous, not a terminal chapter. (b) Terminal sections are USER-DIRECTED and second-person, re-binding everything to the reader's decision: "你当前问题的'最关键结论'（浓缩版）" (the most critical conclusion FOR YOUR question, condensed), "如果你是找职业方向，分别适合谁？", "你可直接拿来用的'公司清单'", "本回答的证据缺口" (the evidence gaps in THIS answer). 38/96 end on a synthesis-type heading; median position of the first synthesis heading is 0.84 through the doc. Several reports even close by OFFERING follow-on deliverables to the user. bodhi treats the report as an answer to a person, not as a document.

Some reports also front-load a verdict ("先给结论" / "Bottom line" / "Executive Summary" as H2), but only ~13/100 -- it is not the dominant pattern.
EVIDENCE CITED: ARTIFACTS (all measurements mine, computed over all 100 reports):
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl (100 records: id, prompt, article)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/bodhi/race_result.txt -> Comprehensiveness 0.5415, Insight 0.5460, Instruction Following 0.5441, Readability 0.5187, Overall 0.5407, N=100/0 failed
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/bodhi/raw_results.jsonl (per-task dimension scores; used for the feature correlations)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv (the board)
Comparison corpora in the same dir: cellcog-max.jsonl, WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl, gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch.jsonl, openai-deepresearch.jsonl, dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl, sourcery.jsonl.

KEY MEASURED NUMBERS: index->URL stable in 99/99 reports; mean max citation index 128.7 vs 61.3 unique URLs cited (50% of slots unused); first citation != [1] in 47/99; corr(doc position, citation index) bodhi +0.279 / WhaleCloud +0.872 / sourcery +0.018; 4.97 cites per 1000 chars; 72% of substantive sentences cited; inference-labeled lines uncited 67% vs other labeled lines uncited 32%; 1720 distinct bold labels / 1955 uses; 1186 H2s with max repeat 4; 66/100 reports have zero tables; 92/100 have no reference list; 3/100 have a limitations heading; conflict markers 4.5/report; bullets 53% of lines, prose 25%; named-study-in-prose 0.78/report (lowest of pack); length 9k-147k chars, stdev/mean 0.56; corr(length, insight) +0.05.

PUBLISHED RECORD (marketing only, no primary technical source exists):
- https://www.publicissapient.com/platforms/bodhi (claims "#1 spot" -- CONTRADICTS their own #3 posts; unreliable)
- https://www.threads.com/@publicissapient/post/DVb9hJAErUy/ ("ranked #3 globally on Hugging Face's DeepResearch-Bench")
- https://www.linkedin.com/posts/dulce-mar%C3%ADa-g%C3%A1mez_the-reasoning-engine-behind-sapient-bodhi-activity-7434886509041823744-knU_ (fetched; zero architecture detail)
- https://www.publicissapient.com/company/news/aws-agentic-ai-specialization (Bedrock + AgentCore)
- Benchmark itself: https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.11763 , https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench
- Searched and found NOTHING: arXiv, GitHub, HF model/dataset cards, WeChat/Zhihu, Chinese-language terms.

Check: does the cited source actually say this? Is it current (2026)? Is an "architecture" being inferred from
output text and then stated as fact? Inference from artifacts is LEGITIMATE but must be LABELLED as inference,
not presented as documented design. Flag any place that line is crossed.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
recs=[json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl')]
print("n=",len(recs), "keys", recs[0].keys())
cit=re.compile(r'\[\[(\d+)\]\]\(([^)]+)\)')
stable=0; tot=0; maxidx=[]; uniq=[]; firstnot1=0; nfirst=0
for r in recs:
    a=r['article']
    ms=cit.findall(a)
    if not ms: continue
    tot+=1
    d={}
    ok=True
    for n,u in ms:
        if n in d and d[n]!=u: ok=False
        d.setdefault(n,u)
    stable+=ok
    maxidx.append(max(int(n) for n,_ in ms))
    uniq.append(len(set(u for _,u in ms)))
    nfirst+=1
    if int(ms[0][0])!=1: firstnot1+=1
print("reports w/ citations",tot,"stable idx->url",stable)
print("mean max idx",round(sum(maxidx)/len(maxidx),1),"median",st.median(maxidx),"max",max(maxidx))
print("mean uniq urls",round(sum(uniq)/len(uniq),1))
print("first cite != [1]:",firstnot1,"/",nfirst)
EOF

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
cit=re.compile(r'\[\[(\d+)\]\]\(([^)]+)\)')
def load(f): return [json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/'+f)]
def corr(x,y):
    n=len(x); mx=sum(x)/n; my=sum(y)/n
    num=sum((a-mx)*(b-my) for a,b in zip(x,y))
    den=(sum((a-mx)**2 for a in x)*sum((b-my)**2 for b in y))**.5
    return num/den if den else 0
for f in ['bodhi.jsonl','WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl','sourcery.jsonl']:
    cs=[];fr=[]
    for r in load(f):
        a=r['article']; L=len(a)
        pos=[(m.start()/L,int(m.group(1))) for m in cit.finditer(a)]
        if len(pos)<5: continue
        cs.append(corr([p for p,_ in pos],[i for _,i in pos]))
        idx=[i for _,i in pos]; rng=max(idx)-min(idx)
        e=[i for p,i in pos if p<=0.2]
        if rng and e: fr.append((max(e)-min(e))/rng)
    print(f, "corr", round(sum(cs)/len(cs),3), "first20%range", round(sum(fr)/len(fr),2))
# bodhi detail
recs=load('bodhi.jsonl')
dens=[];h2=[];h3=[];tbl=0;noref=0;lim=0;lens=[]
h2all={}
bold=re.compile(r'^\s*[-*]?\s*\*\*([^*]{1,60})\*\*\s*[:：]',re.M)
labels={}
for r in recs:
    a=r['article']; lens.append(len(a))
    dens.append(len(cit.findall(a))/max(len(a),1)*1000)
    hs=re.findall(r'^##\s+(.+)$',a,re.M); h2.append(len(hs))
    for h in hs: h2all[h.strip()]=h2all.get(h.strip(),0)+1
    h3.append(len(re.findall(r'^###\s+(.+)$',a,re.M)))
    rows=[l for l in a.split('\n') if l.strip().startswith('|') and l.count('|')>=3]
    if not rows: tbl+=1
    if not re.search(r'^#{1,4}\s*.*(参考文献|References|Reference List|Bibliography|资料来源|来源列表)',a,re.M|re.I): noref+=1
    if re.search(r'^#{1,4}\s*.*(局限|Limitations?|不足)',a,re.M|re.I): lim+=1
    for m in bold.finditer(a): labels[m.group(1).strip()]=labels.get(m.group(1).strip(),0)+1
print("cites/1000ch",round(sum(dens)/len(dens),2))
print("H2 total",sum(h2),"mean",round(sum(h2)/100,1),"max repeat",max(h2all.values()),"H3 mean",round(sum(h3)/100,1))
print("zero-table reports",tbl,"no-refs",noref,"limitations heading",lim)
print("distinct bold labels",len(labels),"uses",sum(labels.values()))
print("len chars min",min(lens),"max",max(lens),"mean",int(st.mean(lens)),"stdev/mean",round(st.stdev(lens)/st.mean(lens),2))
print("mean words(ws+cjk approx)", int(st.mean([len(re.findall(r'\w+',r['article']))for r in recs])))
EOF

content.content:
===== WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl
 | # 中国社会9阶层实际收入、财务状况与中产阶层深度研究报告（2024—2025） |  | --- |  | ## 执行摘要 |  | 本报告采用网络广泛流传的"中国社会9阶层模型"——以政治权力与经济资源的实际掌控能力为核心维度，将中国社会划分为1—9级，从"对全国局势有控制能力"的顶层（1级）到"基本无法自食其力"的底层（9级）。该框架虽为民间模型，但因其直击权力与资源分配本质、引入体制内外轴线，在解读中国政商关系与阶层固化机制方面具有独特的分析价值。 |  | **方法论说明：** 本报告采用混合方法（Mixed Methods），综合运用比较分析（多口径中产定义横比）、结构分析（9阶层收入—资产—负债三维解构）、因果机制分析（中产焦虑的深层根因）、情景分析（中产未来演变路径）和反事实推演（若无房产化财富结构，中产消费倾向将如何变化）。选择原因：研究主题横跨经济学、社会学与政治经济学，单一方法无法覆盖权力—资本—
===== sourcery.jsonl
# 截至2026年中国九阶层实际收入水平与中产阶层财务状况研究报告 |  | ## Table of Contents | - [Executive Summary](#executive-summary) | - [Key Questions Answered](#key-questions-answered) |   - [Q1: 中国居民的实际收入水平如何？](#q1-) |   - [Q2: 中国存在哪些阶层分类框架？](#q2-) |   - [Q3: 如何界定中国的中产阶层？](#q3-) |   - [Q4: 中产阶层实际有多少人？](#q4-) |   - [Q5: 中产阶层的资产与负债结构如何？](#q5-) |   - [Q6: 中产阶层面临哪些结构性压力？](#q6-) | - [Core Findings](#core-findings) |   - [一、中国居民整体收入基本面（2025年官方数据）](#202
===== cellcog-max.jsonl
# 中国阶层结构与中产画像深度研究报告 |  | **发布：2026 年 4 月 16 日 | CellCog 研究** |  | --- |  | ## 引言 |  | 中国社会结构在过去三十年经历了或许是人类经济史上规模最大、速度最快的阶层重塑。然而，每当我们试图用一个简洁的框架去描述这个结构时，立刻会遇到三重困难：**术语不统一**（中产阶级 vs 中等收入群体）、**数据不一致**（七套中产定义规模相差近百倍）、**边界不清晰**（收入、财富、职业、身份错位）。本报告不试图绕开这些困难，而是直面它们——把"困难本身"作为研究对象，用数据驱动的 9 阶层框架，系统化地回答用户提出的核心问题：**中国目前 9 阶层的实际收入与财务状况如何？中产阶级有哪些特点？实际人数与财力如何？** |  | 报告采用"方法论优先"的写作策略：第一章与第二章建立学术地基；第三至第十一章呈现九阶层画像；第十二章深化中产专题；第十三章分析 20

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
def corr(x,y):
    n=len(x); mx=sum(x)/n; my=sum(y)/n
    num=sum((a-mx)*(b-my) for a,b in zip(x,y))
    den=(sum((a-mx)**2 for a in x)*sum((b-my)**2 for b in y))**.5
    return num/den if den else 0
def go(f,pat):
    cs=[];fr=[]
    for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/'+f):
        a=json.loads(l)['article']; L=len(a)
        pos=[(m.start()/L,int(m.group(1))) for m in re.finditer(pat,a)]
        if len(pos)<5: continue
        cs.append(corr([p for p,_ in pos],[i for _,i in pos]))
        idx=[i for _,i in pos]; rng=max(idx)-min(idx)
        e=[i for p,i in pos if p<=0.2]
        if rng and e: fr.append((max(e)-min(e))/rng)
    print(f,"n",len(cs),"corr",round(sum(cs)/len(cs),3),"first20%coverage",round(sum(fr)/len(fr),2))
go('bodhi.jsonl',r'\[\[(\d+)\]\]\([^)]+\)')
go('WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl',r'\[\^?(\d+)\^?\]')
go('sourcery.jsonl',r'\[\^?(\d+)\^?\]')
EOF

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
recs=[json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl')]
cit=re.compile(r'\[\[(\d+)\]\]\([^)]+\)')
dens=[];h2c=[];h3c=[];zt=0;noref=0;lim=0;lens=[];words=[]
h2all={};labels={}
bold=re.compile(r'\*\*([^*\n]{1,60}?)\*\*\s*[:：]')
inf=re.compile(r'含义|机制|解读|判断|启示|implication|why|takeaway|interpretation|意味',re.I)
inf_u=inf_c=oth_u=oth_c=0
for r in recs:
    a=r['article']; lens.append(len(a))
    words.append(len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z0-9]+',a))+len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',a))//1.7 if 0 else 0)
    dens.append(len(cit.findall(a))/len(a)*1000)
    hs=[h.strip() for h in re.findall(r'^##\s+(.+)$',a,re.M)]; h2c.append(len(hs))
    for h in hs: h2all[h]=h2all.get(h,0)+1
    h3c.append(len(re.findall(r'^###\s+(.+)$',a,re.M)))
    if not [l for l in a.split('\n') if l.strip().startswith('|') and l.count('|')>=3]: zt+=1
    if not re.search(r'^#{1,4}\s*.{0,10}(参考文献|References|Bibliography|资料来源|来源)',a,re.M|re.I): noref+=1
    if re.search(r'^#{1,4}\s*.{0,15}(局限|Limitations?)',a,re.M|re.I): lim+=1
    for line in a.split('\n'):
        m=bold.search(line.strip())
        if not m or not line.strip().startswith(('**','- **','* **','#')) : 
            if not m: continue
        lab=m.group(1).strip()
        if not line.strip().startswith(('**','-','*')): continue
        labels[lab]=labels.get(lab,0)+1
        cited=bool(cit.search(line))
        if inf.search(lab):
            if cited: inf_c+=1
            else: inf_u+=1
        else:
            if cited: oth_c+=1
            else: oth_u+=1
print("cites/1000ch",round(st.mean(dens),2))
print("H2 total",sum(h2c),"mean",round(st.mean(h2c),1),"max repeat H2",max(h2all.values()),"H3 mean",round(st.mean(h3c),1))
print("zero-table",zt,"no-ref-section",noref,"limitations-heading",lim)
print("distinct labels",len(labels),"uses",sum(labels.values()))
print("inference labels uncited",inf_u,"cited",inf_c,"-> uncited%",round(100*inf_u/(inf_u+inf_c)))
print("other labels uncited",oth_u,"cited",oth_c,"-> uncited%",round(100*oth_u/(oth_u+oth_c)))
print("chars min",min(lens),"max",max(lens),"stdev/mean",round(st.stdev(lens)/st.mean(lens),2))
EOF

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
def words(a):
    return len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z0-9]+',a))+int(len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',a))/1.5)
for f in ['bodhi.jsonl','cellcog-max.jsonl','gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch.jsonl']:
    w=[words(json.loads(l)['article']) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/'+f)]
    print(f,"mean words~",int(st.mean(w)))
recs=[json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl')]
# label cross-report uniqueness
from collections import Counter
bold=re.compile(r'\*\*([^*\n]{1,60}?)\*\*\s*[:：]')
per=[set(bold.findall(r['article'])) for r in recs]
c=Counter()
for s in per:
    for l in s: c[l.strip()]+=1
multi=sum(1 for k,v in c.items() if v>1)
print("labels appearing in >1 report:",multi,"of",len(c))
# bullets/prose
b=p=0
for r in recs:
    for l in r['article'].split('\n'):
        s=l.strip()
        if not s: continue
        if re.match(r'^([-*+]|\d+[.)])\s',s): b+=1
        elif not s.startswith(('#','|','>')) and len(s)>0: p+=1
tot=b+p
print("bullet lines",b,"prose-ish",p)
# conflict markers
cm=re.compile(r'然而|但是|相互矛盾|矛盾|分歧|争议|however|in contrast|conflict|disagree|discrepan',re.I)
print("conflict markers/report", round(st.mean([len(cm.findall(r['article'])) for r in recs]),1))
# verbatim quotes
qs=["Evidence constraint: I only cite journal articles","公司自述","数据口径","本回答的证据缺口","先给结论","统一解读","不是“谁热”"]
corpus="\n".join(r['article'] for r in recs)
for q in qs: print(repr(q), corpus.count(q))
EOF

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
recs=[json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl')]
first=[]
for r in recs:
    hs=re.findall(r'^##\s+(.+)$',r['article'],re.M)
    first.append(hs[0].strip() if hs else "")
print("first H2 contains 口径:",sum(1 for h in first if '口径' in h))
# verbatim
for r in recs:
    if 'Evidence constraint' in r['article']:
        i=r['article'].find('Evidence constraint')
        print("VERBATIM>>>",r['article'][i-260:i+120].replace('\n',' / '))
# last heading synthesis-type
syn=re.compile(r'结论|建议|清单|怎么做|如果你|你可|takeaway|recommend|bottom line|so what|行动|决策|适合谁|落地|证据缺口|下一步|next step',re.I)
lastsyn=0; pos=[]
for r in recs:
    a=r['article']
    hs=[(m.start()/len(a),m.group(1).strip()) for m in re.finditer(r'^#{2,3}\s+(.+)$',a,re.M)]
    if not hs: continue
    if syn.search(hs[-1][1]): lastsyn+=1
    f=[p for p,h in hs if syn.search(h)]
    if f: pos.append(min(f))
print("last heading synthesis-type:",lastsyn,"/",len(recs))
print("median position of first synthesis heading:",round(st.median(pos),2),"n",len(pos))
# second person
print("reports using 你 in a heading:",sum(1 for r in recs if re.search(r'^#{2,3}.*你',r['article'],re.M)))
# front-load verdict
fl=re.compile(r'先给结论|Bottom line|执行摘要|Executive Summary|先说结论|结论先行|一页式结论|核心结论',re.I)
print("front-loaded verdict in first H2:",sum(1 for h in first if fl.search(h)))
EOF

Bodhi Deep Research ranked #3 on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard, according to some sources. However, other sources on Publicis Sapient's website state that Sapient Bodhi earned the #1 spot on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard for complex, multi-step reasoning.

The #3 ranking appears to be from an earlier announcement (dated March 2026), while the #1 ranking claims appear on more recent pages. This suggests that Sapient Bodhi may have improved its ranking over time from #3 to #1 on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bodhi Deep Research and Publicis Sapient:

## Overview

Bodhi is an enterprise agentic AI platform engineered to put agents into production, with pre-built agents that share a context layer governing how they reason and act inside complex systems, and embeds business rules, workflows and controls directly into every agent.

## Deep Research Performance

Bodhi Deep Research ranked #3 on the Hugging Face DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard, confirming that Sapient Bodhi performs among the best AI agents in advanced, multi-step reasoning and analysis. (Note: Some sources also reference the #1 ranking position.)

## Architecture & Key Features

These AI-powered platforms use a multi-agent architecture with specialized agents that collaborate, learn, and adapt to user feedback, understand business context quickly, and reduce the need for manual intervention.

Bodhi provides governance, orchestration and shared memory across agents, managing how agents interact with data, systems and each other, ensuring consistency, traceability and control as AI scales.

Bodhi is model- and cloud-agnostic by design, integrating with existing enterprise systems, data platforms and AI models without forcing vendor lock-in.

The search results indicate that Publicis Sapient has published information about Bodhi's capabilities and performance, but I did not find a specific technical architecture report document in these results.

cm=re.compile(r'然而|矛盾|分歧|争议|不一致|however|in contrast|conflicting|disagree|discrepan',re.I)
for f in ['bodhi.jsonl','cellcog-max.jsonl','gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch.jsonl','WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl']:
    v=[len(cm.findall(json.loads(l)['article'])) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/'+f)]
    print(f,"conflict markers/report",round(st.mean(v),1))
EOF

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project; head -c 400 drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/bodhi/raw_results.jsonl; echo; python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,statistics as st
raw={}
for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/bodhi/raw_results.jsonl'):
    d=json.loads(l); raw[str(d.get('id',d.get('task_id')))]=d
recs=[json.loads(l) for l in open('drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl')]
def corr(x,y):
    n=len(x);mx=sum(x)/n;my=sum(y)/n
    num=sum((a-mx)*(b-my) for a,b in zip(x,y)); den=(sum((a-mx)**2 for a in x)*sum((b-my)**2 for b in y))**.5
    return num/den if den else 0
scope=re.compile(r'口径|范围|假设|定义|Scope|assumption|说明|界定|术语|先把|Definitions?|Methodology',re.I)
X=[];Y=[];L=[];I=[]
for r in recs:
    k=str(r['id'])
    if k not in raw: continue
    d=raw[k]
    ifs=d.get('instruction_following') or d.get('instruction_following_score')
    ins=d.get('insight') or d.get('insight_score')
    if ifs is None: continue
    hs=re.findall(r'^##\s+(.+)$',r['article'],re.M)
    X.append(1 if hs and scope.search(hs[0]) else 0); Y.append(ifs)
    L.append(len(r['article'])); I.append(ins)
print("n",len(X),"scope-opener n=",sum(X))
print("corr(scope-first-H2, IF) =",round(corr(X,Y),3))
print("mean IF with scope opener",round(st.mean([y for x,y in zip(X,Y) if x]),3),"without",round(st.mean([y for x,y in zip(X,Y) if not x]),3))
print("corr(length, insight)",round(corr(L,I),3))
EOF

content.text: All key numbers reproduced independently. Let me write up.

input.reasoning:
I re-derived every measurable claim from the artifacts myself (fresh scripts over /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/bodhi.jsonl + the three comparison corpora + scores/bodhi/raw_results.jsonl), and separately checked the published record by web search.

WHAT REPRODUCES (essentially exactly — this analysis is unusually honest about its measurements):
- Board: leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows bodhi 54.07, rank 3; race_result.txt gives 0.5415/0.5460/0.5441/0.5187, N=100/0. CONFIRMED.
- Citation index: 99/99 reports with citations have a stable index->URL map (100th has zero citations). Mean max index 128.7, median 121, max 280. Mean unique URLs cited 62.0 (claim: 61.3) => ~52% of numbered slots unused. First citation != [1] in 46/99 (claim: 47/99). CONFIRMED.
- Position-vs-index correlation: bodhi +0.279 / first-20% of doc spans 83% of the index range; WhaleCloud +0.859 / 28% (claim 0.872/27%); sourcery +0.019 / 93% (claim 0.018/96%). Note the comparison systems use a different citation syntax ([n], not [[n]](url)), so the regex must be varied per system — the claimant evidently did this. CONFIRMED.
- 4.97 citations per 1000 chars: exact. 1186 H2s, max H2 repeat = 4, 11.9 H2 / 29.1 H3 per report: exact. 66/100 zero-table reports: exact. Length 8,954–147,533 chars, stdev/mean 0.56: exact.
- Two-register claim: with my own bold-label regex I get inference-labelled lines uncited 79% (95/121) vs other labelled lines uncited 34% (501/1469) — the claimed ~2x gap is real and if anything understated. CONFIRMED directionally.
- Per-report rubric: 1780 distinct bold labels; only 18 of them appear in more than one report. This is the strongest single result in the whole analysis. CONFIRMED.
- Terminal synthesis: last heading is synthesis-type in 38/100 (claim 38/96); front-loaded verdict in first H2 in exactly 13/100. Second-person headings in 25/100. CONFIRMED.
- corr(length, insight) = +0.024 (claim +0.05) — i.e. zero. "Length is not the lever" CONFIRMED. Word-count absolutes (10.7k/23.9k/16.9k) are tokenizer-dependent — my CJK-adjusted count gives 9.1k/18.5k/13.6k — but the ordering and the ~2x gap to cellcog hold.
- Published record: web search confirms marketing-only sourcing (product page, Threads, LinkedIn, AWS specialization page), no arXiv/GitHub/HF card/tech report, and the live #1-vs-#3 contradiction between publicissapient.com/platforms/bodhi and their own social posts. Multi-agent / shared context layer / shared memory / model-agnostic are indeed the only architectural nouns they publish. CONFIRMED.

WHERE IT FAILS OR OVERREACHES (this is why PARTIAL, not CONFIRMED):

1. FALSIFIED SUB-CLAIM. "[The scope contract] is almost certainly what drives instruction_following 54.41." Within bodhi's own 100, reports whose first H2 is a scope/definition/口径 section score mean IF 0.547 vs 0.544 for those without; corr = +0.047. There is no within-corpus signal that the scope section drives the IF score. The causal attribution is unsupported by the very artifacts it is drawn from.

2. FREQUENCY OVERSTATED in claim (3). "Reports open not with an executive summary but with a definitional/measurement-convention contract... First H2 is very often a 口径 section." Actual: 口径 appears in the first H2 of only 7/100 reports; only 36/100 reports contain the word 口径 anywhere; a broad scope/definition/assumption regex matches the first H2 in 18/100; and 13/100 open with an executive-summary/verdict H2 — which the claim itself elsewhere concedes. The verbatim examples are genuine (I found "**Evidence constraint**: I only cite journal articles." in the corpus), but "Evidence constraint" occurs exactly once in 100 reports. The real, defensible pattern is weaker: bodhi's opener is usually a framing/definitional move ("先把概念说清", "先把比较框架做实", "研究边界", "…到底是什么：边界、谱系") rather than a measurement-convention contract per se.

3. NOT REPRODUCIBLE AS STATED. "Conflict markers 4.5/report vs cellcog 12.4, gemini 8.3, WhaleCloud 8.6." With my own marker set I get bodhi 1.4, WhaleCloud 3.5, cellcog 4.6, gemini 7.1. The rank claim (bodhi has the fewest) survives; the absolute numbers and the cellcog/gemini ordering do not, and the marker list is never specified, so the number is not checkable. Similarly "3/100 have a limitations heading" -> I get 7/100 with a slightly broader regex; "92/100 no reference list" -> I get 89/100. These are definition-sensitive, not wrong, but they are stated with false precision.

4. THE LINE BETWEEN OBSERVATION AND MECHANISM IS CROSSED IN THREE PLACES. The document does carry a global disclaimer ("Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure") and honestly marks item (8) UNVERIFIED — that is real intellectual hygiene and most of the write-up stays on the right side. But:
   (a) (1) "So numbering is NOT order-of-first-appearance -- it is assigned by a research phase that runs BEFORE composition, which builds a ~130-280-item numbered library, of which the writer selects about half." The observation is confirmed; the pipeline is an inference stated as fact. An equally consistent mechanism: citations are renumbered post-hoc against a global source pool by a citation manager, with no separate "curate" step, or a planner pre-assigns sources per section.
   (b) (2) "Decisive test... bodhi's near-flat correlation MEANS the writer had the entire source pool addressable from sentence one." A +0.279 correlation is evidence against append-as-you-go numbering; it is not decisive between "one composer holds the library" and "sources were assigned to sections up front by a planner". "Decisive" and "means" are too strong.
   (c) "The bold label LICENSES an uncited, cross-source inferential sentence" and "bodhi INVENTS A TASK-SPECIFIC ANALYTIC RUBRIC per report" are mechanism narratives laid over what is, in the data, a correlation between label type and citedness plus a label-uniqueness count. Both are good hypotheses; neither is a documented design.

BOTTOM LINE: no primary source exists, so nothing here can be confirmed as documented design — and the claim says so. The artifact measurements are real and I reproduced ~15 of them to 2 significant figures. The defects are (i) one falsified causal attribution (scope section -> instruction_following), (ii) one materially overstated frequency (口径/scope opener), (iii) unspecified-and-unreproducible conflict-marker counts, and (iv) three places where inferred pipeline mechanism is written in the declarative voice of documented architecture despite the global disclaimer.
input.corrected:
Corrections to fold back in:

(3) SCOPE CONTRACT AS SECTION ZERO — restate as: "Reports typically open with a framing/definitional move rather than an executive summary (only 13/100 front-load a verdict H2). A measurement-convention (口径) opener is a recognizable bodhi signature but is NOT the dominant pattern: 口径 appears in the first H2 of 7/100 reports and anywhere in 36/100; a broad scope/definition/assumption match on the first H2 covers 18/100. The verbatim 'Evidence constraint: I only cite journal articles' occurs once in the corpus." DELETE "This is almost certainly what drives instruction_following 54.41" — it is falsified within the corpus: mean IF for scope-opener reports 0.547 vs 0.544 without, corr = +0.047.

(1) Restate as observation + labelled inference: "OBSERVED: index->URL mapping is stable in 99/99 reports; mean max index 128.7 vs 62.0 unique URLs cited (~52% of slots never used); first citation is not [1] in 46/99. Numbering is therefore not order-of-first-appearance. INFERRED (not documented): a research/collection phase produces a global numbered source pool before or independently of composition, of which roughly half is cited. An alternative consistent with the same data is post-hoc renumbering against a global pool."

(2) Soften "decisive": "OBSERVED: corr(doc position, citation index) = +0.279 for bodhi (WhaleCloud +0.859, sourcery +0.019); the first 20% of a bodhi report already spans 83% of its index range (WhaleCloud 28%). INFERRED: the composer addresses the whole source pool rather than accumulating sources section-by-section. This rules out append-as-encountered numbering; it does not distinguish a single whole-library composer from a planner that pre-assigns sources to sections."

CONFLICT HANDLING — replace the unreproducible absolutes: "bodhi uses the fewest explicit conflict markers of the top systems (rank confirmed under multiple marker sets; absolute per-report counts vary with the marker list — with a fixed set I measure bodhi 1.4, WhaleCloud 3.5, cellcog 4.6, gemini 7.1). 7/100 (not 3/100) carry a limitations-type heading; 89/100 (not 92/100) have no reference section."

TWO-REGISTER FINDING — keep, and strengthen: independently reproduced. Inference-labelled bold lines are uncited 79% of the time (95/121) vs 34% (501/1469) for all other bold-labelled lines — a >2x gap. Per-report rubric confirmed: 1780 distinct bold labels across 100 reports, of which only 18 appear in more than one report.

LENGTH — keep the conclusion, flag the units: corr(length, insight) = +0.024 within bodhi's 100 (my count), i.e. zero. Word-count absolutes depend on CJK tokenization (bodhi ~9-11k, cellcog ~18-24k, gemini ~14-17k); the invariant is that bodhi is the shortest of the top group by roughly 2x versus cellcog.

Everything else in the claim (board position, index stability, 4.97 cites/1000 chars, 1186 H2s with max repeat 4, 66/100 zero-table, stdev/mean 0.56, 38/100 terminal synthesis heading, 13/100 front-loaded verdict, marketing-only published record with a live #1-vs-#3 self-contradiction) reproduced exactly and stands.
